title: Color Palette — Theme Colors
description: Replace hard-coded color values with CSS custom properties (variables) for easy theming. Define --primary-color, --text-color, --bg-color, and --accent-color in a :root rule, then use var() references throughout your existing CSS. This makes it trivial to change the entire blog's color scheme by editing just four values.
test_code:
assert ':root' in css.lower(), 'Missing :root selector for custom properties'
assert '--primary' in css.lower(), 'Missing --primary-color custom property'
assert '--text' in css.lower(), 'Missing --text-color custom property'
assert '--bg' in css.lower(), 'Missing --bg-color custom property'
assert 'var(' in css.lower(), 'Must use var() to reference custom properties'
acceptance_criteria: var() is used to reference custom properties in existing rules
The visual result should be identical to the previous step
hints: Replace color values like color: var(--text-color);
You can define as many custom properties as you like — consider adding --accent-color for blockquote borders
